"It's just absurd to think that 13-year-olds aren't having sex and they're not pursuing it in a very deliberate, knowing way," she said. "It's absurd." Doran called her out on that one too, saying it's not relevant because of her own age at the time. "There are cases where there could be abuse, so you have to have a law that acts as a deterrent," Mary added. "There are people who might pursue and abuse a situation." Mary Jo Buttafuoco Admits She 'Made a Lot of Mistakes' 25 Years After Amy Fisher Scandal on 'Snapped' View Story In a solo interview, Fualaau said that what she did was legally "wrong," and added that they "maybe should have waited" to have a physical relationship. "I think about it, could I have ever done that, could I have ever been with a girl that's 13 years old right now and I don't see myself doing it," the now-35-year-old said. Speaking by himself, Fualaau also admitted to thinking about separation. "Every now and then, I don't want to talk about that," he added. When Doran asked if he had any advice for his 13-year-old self, Fualaau said with a laugh, "Don't do it!" However, he then went on to say he regrets nothing because of his two children. "Anything that's already happened is already meant to be. it's my journey in life, so I gotta live it out," he added. Dancing with the Stars: Athletes Season 26 Cast Revealed | PEOPLE.com Tonya Harding, Adam Rippon and Mirai Nagasu Join Dancing with the Stars: Athletes Subscribe Search News Photos Royals Gwyneth Paltrow Kim Kardashian Style Watch PeopleTV Subscribe Search Close Home Subscribe News Photos Down Star Tracks Watch PeopleTV Newsletters Royals Down News Meghan Markle Kate Middleton Parents Down Births Expecting Celeb Blogs Style Down News Fashion Beauty Shopping TV Down Kardashians DWTS Housewives The Bachelor Movies Down News Movie Awards Celebrities Music Down Kanye West Beyoncé Carrie Underwood Taylor Swift Human Interest Down News Tragedy & Triumph Heroes Crime Down News Courts & Trials Cold Cases Exclusives Food Down Recipes Food News Celebrity Food Chefs Home & Travel Down Stars at Home Real Estate Décor Celebrity Vacations Health Down Health News Body Positivity Celebrity Fitness Weight Loss Inspiration Pets Down Dogs Cats Adoption Funny Videos Celeb Pets Politics Down Celebrity & Politics Donald Trump Crosswords Magazine Down Print Magazine Give a Gift Premium Customer Service Stay Connected Dancing with the Stars Tonya Harding, Adam Rippon and Mirai Nagasu Join Dancing with the Stars: Athletes Brianne Tracy April 13, 2018 08:27 AM Get ready to see some of your favorite athletes like never before. The cast of Dancing with the Stars : Athletes was announced Friday on Good Morning America , and the competition is about to heat up on the dance floor. During this shortened four-week cycle, 10 teams of athletes and pro dancers will go head – to-head for the coveted Mirrorball trophy. Here is the full list of pairings: Tonya Harding with Sasha Farber Craig Sjodin/ABC It’s official! After much speculation , the most controversial name in figure skating — Tonya Harding — will be joining the show this season. She will be paired with pro dancer and newlywed Sasha Farber , who recently married his fellow pro Emma Slater in March. Frazer Harrison/Getty A source who has known Harding, 47, for years previously told PEOPLE that she’s capable of learning to ballroom dance, but risks being compared to skating rival Nancy Kerrigan , who appeared on season 24 of DWTS last year, placing sixth. Still, “Tonya gets ratings. God knows, she gets ratings,” the source said. Kerrigan , 48, was clubbed on the leg at a practice before the 1994 national skating championships — beginning a bizarre saga that ultimately ended with Harding’s ban from the U.S. Figure Skating Association and her pleading guilty to conspiring to hinder the prosecution of those responsible for the attack. The infamous attack on Kerrigan brought Harding back into the spotlight thanks to the Oscar-nominated film I, Tonya. Adam Rippon with Jenna Johnson Craig Sjodin/ABC Adam Rippon , the first openly gay U.S. Olympian who won a bronze medal during the 2018 Winter Olympics in PyeongChang, will be taking notes from his partner Jenna Johnson on the show. “If the opportunity came up, I would love to do it,” the 28-year-old told People Now of joining DWTS in March , adding that he thought it “would be fun.” Valery Sharifulin/TASS/Getty Though he’s no stranger to performing in front of crowds, Rippon said at the time that there’s one thing about being on the television show that he thinks would be difficult for him. “I’ve never danced with a partner before,” he said. “So that would definitely be something challenging.” “I’m worried because I just don’t know what I’m doing,” he continued. “So we’ll see. If the opportunity came there’d be a lot that I would need to do.” Mirai Nagasu with Alan Bersten Craig Sjodin/ABC Another standout figure skater from the Winter Olympics will be joining the series — Mirai Nagasu. Nagasu made history in February by becoming the first U.S. woman to land the notoriously tricky triple axel in an Olympic competition. After she finished 10th overall in the women’s individual figure skating final, Nagasu made comments to reporters about wanting to compete on DWTS — and now she’s making it a reality alongside pro Alan Bersten. “I smiled in the middle of my program, which is really rare for me. So I enjoyed myself and I thought of this as my audition for Dancing with the Stars, ” Nagasu, 24, said. Jokingly, she later explained her desire to be on the show: “I want to be a star.” Jamie Anderson with Artem Chigvintsev Craig Sjodin/ABC Snowboarder Jamie Anderson will be trading in her snowboard for dancing shoes. She’ll take lessons with pro Artem Chigvintsev. The 27-year-old made the most of her first Winter Olympics in 2014 by earning a gold medal in the slopestyle snowboarding event in Sochi. And she hasn’t slowed down since, earning gold and silver at the annual Winter X Games in 2015, 2016 and 2017. Anderson — the fifth of eight siblings — has said the best advice she’s received is “find something you’re passionate about and really pursue it.” During the 2018 Winter Olympics, Anderson cruised her way to another gold medal and kept her title as the only woman to hold Olympic gold in snowboarding slopestyle. Kareem Abdul-Jabbar with Lindsay Arnold Craig Sjodin/ABC NBA legend Kareem Abdul-Jabbar will team up with the show’s reigning champion Lindsay Arnold , who won last season with Jordan Fisher. The towering 7’2″ 70-year-old still holds the record as the leading point scorer in basketball. After retiring from basketball in 1989, Abdul-Jabbar has become an advocate for social justice issues and was awarded the Presidential Medal of Freedom by President Barack Obama in 2016. Arike Ogunbowale with Gleb Savchenko Notre Dame’s national championship hero Arike Ogunbowale led her team to victory and made the cover of Sports Illustrated . The 21-year-old will take on the competition with pro Gleb Savchenko. Chris Mazdzer with Witney Carson Craig Sjodin/ABC Chris Mazdzer won a silver medal in men’s singles luge at the Winter Olympics and became the first American to ever make the podium in that event. He’ll be taking lessons from pro Witney Carson. Want to keep up on the latest from PEOPLE? Sign up for our daily newsletter to get our best stories of the day delivered straight to your inbox. Jennie Finch Daigle with Keo Motsepe Craig Sjodin/ABC Former pro softball player Jennie Finch Daigle, 37, helped Team USA win a softball gold medal in 2004 and silver in 2008, and now she’ll be trying to score the DWTS winner’s title with pro Keo Motsepe. Johnny Damon with Emma Slater Craig Sjodin/ABC Retired professional baseball outfielder Johnny Damon played in the Major League from 1995 to 2012. Now he’ll be teaming up with Slater to try to hit a home run when it comes to the judges’ scores. Damon, 44, met a bit of controversy in 2005 when he signed with the Yankees. After years of playing with the Red Sox, he was subsequently vilified by many Boston fans because of the team’s rivalry. Josh Norman with Sharna Burgess Craig Sjodin/ABC Josh Norman is a 30-year-old cornerback for the Washington Redskins, and was drafted by the Carolina Panthers in the fifth round of the 2012 NFL Draft after playing college football at Coastal Carolina. Many consider him to be one of the best cornerbacks in the NFL, and he’ll be paired with pro Sharna Burgess on the show. Season 26 of Dancing with the Stars premieres Monday, April 30 at 8 p.m. ET on ABC. How To Audition | NBC The Voice - Official Casting & Audition Site 2018 Audition Cities How To Audition Video Submission Eligibility FAQs Audition Tips How To Audition Step 1: Create an Artist Account Sign Up HERE at nbcthevoice.com to create your Artist Account. You will receive an Artist Account number and once you've confirmed your e-mail address, you'll be able to customize your Artist Profile and register for an audition city. Step 2: Choose An Audition City Once you've created your Artist Account, you must select the city you'd like to attend for the Open Call Audition. You will be able to select a city, date, and time group of your choice. Only come to the audition on the day & time you've registered for. Step 3: Print Your Artist Audition Pass ONE WEEK before your audition day, you will receive your personal Artist Audition Pass. It will be e-mailed to you. You will also be able to download your pass by logging into your Artist Account. You MUST PRINT and BRING your Artist Audition Pass along with a photo ID in order to audition. Step 4: Auditions Your selected city, date, time & location will be printed on your Artist Audition Pass. Only attend your selected audition day and time. DO NOT FORGET: Your printed Artist Audition Pass and your Photo ID. Open Call Guidelines Please prepare two (2) songs for your open call audition.  You will be required to perform one song, but have the second song ready if asked. A CAPPELLA ONLY at the open call auditions. NO background tracks, NO accompaniment, NO instruments. The only thing we want to hear is YOUR VOICE. To be clear, you CANNOT play your guitar at the Open Call Audition. Songs can be by any artist and from any genre (pop, rock, R&B, hip-hop, alternative, latin, country, blues, indie). Pick songs that fit your voice and best represent the artist you are. Family, friends, managers, vocal instructors, etc. will NOT be allowed at the audition. If you are under 18 or are otherwise a minor in your home state, one (1) parent or guardian will be allowed with you at the audition. If you require special accommodations due to a disability or other need, please contact our casting team prior to your audition. Come dressed in a style that represents you as an artist. Do NOT come in "chicken costumes." We are looking for serious artists ONLY. Please plan to be at the audition all day.  We suggest bringing snacks and water.  Please check the weather in your audition city and plan accordingly as some audition venues have outside waiting areas. The coaches will NOT be at the open call auditions. You may be asked to stay for a call back please plan accordingly. Callback Audition Guidelines (This is ONLY if you get a Red Card at the Open Call) You may be asked to a callback. If you are selected, you will be scheduled a time & date to return. Here are the guidelines for the callback audition. NOTE: This is ONLY for artists that audition at the open call and are given a callback. You must prepare three (3) current/popular songs for the callback. You may be asked to perform a song of the producer's choice. NO original music. You may sing to a backing track, play an instrument, have one (1) person accompany you. A cappella is NOT allowed at the CALLBACKS; you must have accompaniment. If you play an instrument while you sing, you must perform at least one (1) song without. The coaches will NOT be at the callback auditions. Artist Account Login/Register ?? DJ skins, rumours, 'party' map change? Fortnite Season 6 theme REVEALED with new update? DJ skins, rumours, 'party' map change? EPIC is about to start a new Season of Fortnite, and as to be expected, we're already seeing the developer tease strange new things. Here's everything we know about Season 6 so far. 0 By Dom Peppiatt / Published 24th September 2018 EPIC Fortnite Cube and Season 6 NEWS: Epic Games New Battle Pass, Skins and release date info? (Pic: EPIC) Fortnite Season 6 - What happens next? We've just seen a new update from Epic that's given us our first concrete tease of Fortnite Season 6. "All great parties need a DJ," reads the tweet, featuring a picture of a llama decked out like a cyber DJ (think Deadmau5/Daft Punk). "3 days to Season 6," the tweet concludes. This is a hint - are we getting a party theme, a music theme? Is the lake going to turn into a massive party volcano? To get all the information you need, you can head to our Fortnite Season 6 countdown . Related Articles Fallout 76 will always be an online game, Bethesda's Pete Hines confirms Games with Gold September 2018: Xbox BIG News ahead of Major Nelson Free games reveal Fortnite Starter Pack 3: Ace Skin Release Date LEAKED by PS4 Store as Wingman Skin leaves DS (Pic: DS) Fortnite Season 6 is due to start soon - and ahead of the change from Season 5 to Season 6, we're expecting to see Epic tease the new content. Where the Season 4/5 crossover saw a massive rift open in the sky, the 5/6 period has seen a massive mysterious cube enter the map and move around of its own accord. Between this and some other rifts opening up here and there - damaging players with lightning bolts - it's safe to say we're well on the way to seeing more Season 6 changes occur in-game. Below is our complete guide to all things Fortnite Season 6 - we'll update it as more information is revealed. DS (Pic: DS) Fortnite Season 6 - When will it start? Epic hasn't officially announced a start date for Season 6... yet. However, it appears that thanks to the length of Seasons so far, we can expect Season 6 to begin on September 20 . We can extrapolate that information based on what we know about the length of Seasons (Season 5 started on July 12, and they tend to last about two months) Epic is usually a little bit flexible with the dates new Seasons start on though, thanks to the work that goes into the backend of the game when the changeovers occur. So depending on the weight of the work the developers need to put in, we could be seeing more (or less!) of a wait time. Fortnite Skins: Update 5.3 LEAKED Skins and costmetics coming to the shop SOON Take a look at some upcoming Fortnite cosmetics found in the latest Patch v5.3 files 1 / 17 DS (Pic: DS) Fortnite Season 6 - What's with the Cube? As of last month, a giant purple Cube entered Fortnite and has been rolling around the map, confusing players as it goes. The bizarre item surely holds the clues to what's coming next in the game... To understand the Cube, you need to understand Fortnite 'lore' so far. It began when a missile launched and tore a rift open in the sky. Then, from the rifts, new areas came to the game and a giant temple celebrating a tomato-based God appeared, too. The Enforcer skin dropped, which hinted at putting a stop to these space/time shenanigans, and the rift began to close. But as it closed, it summoned a sentient Cube. The Cube - which punishes those that shoot at it with deadly bolts of purple lightning - moves every two hours towards an undisclosed destination. Datamined information suggests the Cube is en route to Loot Lake, but as of right now, its motivations and destination remain a mystery. The Cube made its way to Loot Lake and melted, creating a big bouncy lake - and that's where we're at now. DS (Pic: DS) Fortnite Season 6 - What is the theme? We haven't been told what's going on with Season 6 just yet, but there are a few clues. In a recent tease for the Capoeira emote, Epic seemed to let slip that a castle will be joining the game (you can just about see it in the background, very far away). This, paired with the 'Crazy Castle' spray introduced in the game as part of the Season 5 Battle Pass, suggests we may be getting a fantasy/Medieval theme. That would continue the time-travel narrative Epic has introduced so far - maybe the Cube will zap the map back into the past? Who knows at this point... Epic likes to tease and keep players on their toes, so we're excited to see what comes next. Related Articles Resident Evil 2 Remake: Claire Redfield and RE engine shines in latest Gamescom preview Battlefield 5: Battle Royale mode, new single-player level TEASED in new Gamescom trailer The Division 2: Ubisoft Reveals How They Settled On Washington D.C for Open-World sequel DS (Pic: DS) Fortnite Season 6 - What Skins will be introduced? Every new Season sees the introduction of new skins. At the time of writing, we've not had any leaks or datamines that reveal what sort of skins we'll be seeing as of the start of Season 6. Maybe if we do go back in time, we can expect to see Medieval knights and dragon-themed skins. Maybe we can see mysterious Mayan or Aztec skins. Maybe Neanderthals. Who knows? Perhaps the space/time Enforcer thing will be explored more, too - we'll update our gallery and include new information in this page as more infomation presents itself. Fortnite 5.2 SKINS LEAKED: Update 5.20 Patch reveals NEW skins coming to shop very soon Take a look at some upcoming Fortnite cosmetics found in the latest Patch v5.2 files 1 / 32 Fortnite Season 6 - Hats Rumours abound that Fortnite will be getting hats soon, too. A set of leaks taken from datamined files of the newest build of the game suggests makeovers to older costumes could be coming to the game, supported by new custom headwear. Data suggested that ???favorite_hat,??? ???favorite_vehicledeco,??? and ???favorite_mapmarker??? would be introduced to the game. Coming off the back of the Tomato head that was introduced to the game recently, it would make sense to see more custom head items introduced - and for Epic it makes sense: more items to unlock means more rewards for players, and another revenue stream introduced to the game, too. DS (Pic: DS) Fortnite Season 6 - Custom Battle Bus In the datamine for patch 5.40, players have managed to determine that different Battle Buses will be coming to the game. Thanks to a text field in the title, we learned Fortnite will: "Randomly chooses between favorite Battle Buses (or all owned Battle Buses if nothing is marked as a favorite)" One example we've seen so far is a Purple Battle bus (which we know about thanks to game files) though we've not seen it yet. DS (Pic: DS) Fortnite Season 6 - Weapon Skins The same patch revealed that we'll see a large number of high profile weapons get custom skin options, too. This is something that's been requested heavily by the Fortnite community and is a great addition to the game - despite having a third-person view that obscures your weapon from your own view most of the time. No skin examples have been leaked just yet, but we're expecting to hear more about them soon. DS (Pic: DS) Fortnite Season 6 - New Consumables Part of the version 5.40 datamine has revealed a new consumable items called 'Balloons'. From the text supporting the item, it appears the consumable helps players gracefully fall, float and possibly fly - similar to the Hop Rocks item we've seen before. 'Rapid inflate balloons to help break your fall or soar to new heights!' reads the item description. We're intrigued to see how this is going to work in-game. AMBER Alert - Frequently Asked Questions Skip Navigation (press 2) Home FAQs Site Index Contact Us Search Frequently Asked Questions Where and why did AMBER Alert first start? How does it work? How effective has it been? Who is the National Coordinator for AMBER Alert and what is the Coordinator's role? How does the AMBER Alert plan help children and families? What are the criteria for issuing AMBER Alerts? How are AMBER Alert appropriations spent? Can AMBER Alerts be issued across state and jurisdictional lines? Are AMBER Alerts issued for all missing children? What can I do if my child goes missing? Are there additional resources for law enforcement to use when they receive the report of a critically missing child? What is the Wireless Emergency Alert program? Where and why did AMBER Alert first start? The AMBER Alert System began in 1996 when Dallas-Fort Worth broadcasters teamed with local police to develop an early warning system to help find abducted children.
AMBER stands for America's Missing: Broadcast Emergency Response and was created as a legacy to 9-year-old Amber Hagerman, who was kidnapped while riding her bicycle in Arlington, Texas, and then brutally murdered. Other states and communities soon set up their own AMBER plans as the idea was adopted across the nation. How does it work? Once law enforcement has determined that a child has been abducted and the abduction meets AMBER Alert criteria, law enforcement notifies broadcasters and state transportation officials. AMBER Alerts interrupt regular programming and are broadcast on radio and television and DOT highway signs. AMBER Alerts can also be re-disseminated through lottery, digital billboards, Internet Ad exchanges, Internet Service Providers, Internet search engines, as well as wireless devices such as mobile phones. How effective has it been? As of December 23, 2015 there have been 800 children rescued and returned specifically because of AMBER Alert. AMBER Alerts also serve as deterrents to those who would prey upon our children. AMBER Alert cases have shown that some perpetrators release the abducted child after hearing the AMBER Alert. Who is the National Coordinator for AMBER Alert and what is the Coordinator's role? The Assistant Attorney General for the Office of Justice Programs, U.S. Department of Justice, serves as the National AMBER Alert Coordinator. The role of the  National Coordinator is to facilitate the development of the AMBER network, support the development of state AMBER plans and efforts, help eliminate geographic gaps in AMBER networks, provide regional AMBER network coordination and provide guidance on criteria for issuing an AMBER Alert. How does the AMBER Alert plan help children and families? The establishment of AMBER Alert plans in all 50 states, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands and the expansion of the program into Indian Country and our northern and southern borders mark an important milestone in our efforts to prevent child abductions. No matter where a child is abducted, communities and law enforcement work together to recover missing children quickly and safely. As of December 23, 2015, 800 children have been recovered because of the AMBER Alert system.  AMBER Alert is making a difference in saving children's lives. What are the criteria for issuing AMBER Alerts? Each state AMBER Alert plan includes its own criteria for issuing AMBER Alerts. The PROTECT Act, passed in 2003, which established the role of AMBER Alert Coordinator within the Department of Justice, calls for the Department of Justice to issue minimum standards or guidelines for AMBER Alerts that states can adopt voluntarily. The Department's Guidance on Criteria for Issuing AMBER Alerts is as follows: There is reasonable belief by law enforcement that an abduction has occurred. The law enforcement agency believes that the child is in imminent danger of serious bodily injury or death. There is enough descriptive information about the victim and the abduction for law enforcement to issue an AMBER Alert to assist in the recovery of the child. The abduction is of a child aged 17 years or younger. The child's name and other critical data elements, including the Child Abduction flag, have been entered into the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) system. Most states' guidelines adhere closely to the Department of Justice's recommended guidelines. How are AMBER Alert appropriations spent? To date, the Department of Justice has utilized appropriated funds to create an AMBER Alert communications network and provide the states and territories with training and technical assistance to develop and enhance their AMBER Alert plans. Can AMBER Alerts be issued across state and jurisdictional lines? Absolutely.  When an AMBER Alert is issued an abductor may take the child outside the jurisdiction of the issuing law enforcement agency, crossing state boundaries.  When that occurs, the AMBER Coordinator contacts the State AMBER Coordinator where the abducted child is believed to be and requests the AMBER Alert be extended into their jurisdiction.  This is referred to as a Multi-state AMBER Alert activation. Are AMBER Alerts issued for all missing children? AMBER Alerts are issued for abducted children that meet the AMBER Alert criteria. AMBER Alert is only one tool that law enforcement can use to find abducted children. AMBER Alerts are used in the most serious cases that meet the AMBER criteria. Overuse of AMBER Alert could result in the public becoming desensitized to Alerts when they are issued. What can I do if my child goes missing? If your child goes missing you should immediately contact your local law enforcement agency.  After you have reported your child missing to law enforcement, call the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children at 1-800-THE-LOST (1-800-843-5678) . More information about available resources can be found at http://www.missingkids.org/MissingChild . Are there additional resources for law enforcement to use when they receive the report of a critically missing child? The Department of Justice launched an initiative in 2006 to train Child Abduction Response Teams (CART) nationwide which are designed to assist local law enforcement agencies when they respond to incidents of missing and abducted children.  The teams include regional law enforcement investigators, forensic experts, AMBER Alert coordinators, search and rescue professionals, policy makers, crime intelligence analysts, victim service providers and other interagency resources. AMBER Alerts are issued when a child abduction meets the specific AMBER Alert criteria, however, CART can be used for all missing children's cases.  To date, there are 99 Operational Ready teams and 3799 team members trained representing 45 states, District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, the Bahamas, and Canada. What is the Wireless Emergency Alert program? The Wireless Emergency Alert program is operated by the Federal Emergency Management Agency. It distributes notifications from authorized federal, state, local and tribal government agencies that alert customers with capable devices of imminent threats to safety or an emergency situation. The messages are intended as a supplement to the existing Emergency Alert System, which broadcasts alerts over radio and television.  In addition to AMBER Alerts, the program includes National Weather Service, Presidential and imminent threat alerts.  If you own a capable mobile device, you will automatically receive these alerts when you are in the geographic area where an alert has been issued.  For more information please refer to http://www.missingkids.org/gethelpnow/amber/wea . StyleCaster People Are Mommy-Shaming Cindy Crawford After Kaia Gerber Took a Near-Topless Selfie Facebook Pinterest Twitter Tumblr 0 by Jason Pham 96 Shares 1 year ago Photo: Getty Images Kaia Gerber may come from supermodel blood, but a recent selfie of hers is likely not getting the kind of attention she predicted. The 15-year-old sparked major outrage after she posted a near-topless photo of herself on Instagram—but instead of the backlash being directed at her, it’s actually pointed at her supermodel mom, Cindy Crawford . MORE: 10 of Kaia Gerber’s Absolute Best Hair and Makeup Moments Yup. The mommy-shamers are in full force again and this time their target is the 51-year-old supermodel. Here’s what happened: A few days ago, Gerber, like the teenager she is, felt like taking a selfie, so she headed to the bathroom for its A-plus lighting. For the most part, the selfie looked fine. Gerber’s smokey makeup was on-point and her newly cut shag hair was perfectly tousled. Where the controversy came in is with what Gerber was wearing and how she posed. In the photo, Gerber sported an oversized white bathrobe, which she draped loosely over her shoulders. Like people are under bathrobes, the up-and-coming model likely was nude, which is why the draped robe appeared to fall just shy of exposing her breasts. Credit: Instagram | @kaiagerber MORE: Kaia Gerber’s New Shag Haircut Is Giving Us Serious Summer Vibes Though no private parts were visible in Gerber’s photo, that didn’t stop the Internet’s mommy-shamers from coming down hard on her famous mom’s parenting choices. Considering Gerber’s age, a majority of the comments slammed Crawford for allowing a 15-year-old to take such a racy selfie. “I love Kaia with all my heart and she is gorgeous, but she is too young for this type of photo,” one said. “It was a matter of time… but I still hate to see this. Cindy — take the reins now before you can’t anymore,” another added. “Is she seriously 15?!! How can her parents allow her to exploit herself in this way,” a person commented. “Her parents don’t care,” another chimed. “Lack of good parenting results in this false glorification of the self… she doesn’t look like a 15-year-old girl. She looks like a woman, she teases like one, and she probably behaves like one. It’s all about the external image, an empty shell. They are craving for the wrong attention!” While Gerber’s selfie is racier than we would typically expect from a 15-year-old, hardcore mommy-shaming is never the solution. Love forever,  Stacy Shain Sponsored Bella Thorne Says Freeform Called Her ‘Fat’ & ‘Ugly’ While Filming ‘Famous in Love’ Facebook Pinterest Twitter Tumblr 0 by Jason Pham 1 Shares 18 hours ago Photo: Axelle/Bauer-Griffin/FilmMagic. When Bella Thorne’s Freeform show, Famous in Love , was cancelled in June, fans suspected she was feuding with the show’s creator, I. Marlene King, after she learned the news via Twitter instead of a phone call. Turns out, Thorne wasn’t feuding with King (whom she had a “friendly and professional relationship” with) but she did have a problem with Freeform, whom she claims called her “fat” and “ugly.” In an interview with The Los Angeles Times , the 21-year-old actor claimed Freeform verbally abused her twice while filming Famous in Love , which she starred in for two seasons. Once was via e-mails and the other was on set, with her cast watching. “One issue is when you have a girl, a young girl who is on your show, you can’t tell her that she’s ugly or she’s fat or she’s this or she’s that because that’s going to make her feel bad,” Thorne said. When asked to specify who called her “ugly,” Thorne named the network. She claimed the abuse was supposed to stop in the second season but never did. “In e-mails, and there was another thing that was in front of everyone on set,” Thorne said. “After, the cast was texting me, ‘Oh, my God, Bella. The Apprentice's Karren teases this year's candidates The Apprentice's Karren teases this year's candidates Share 11 hours ago 1:19 AM By Sam Ashurst 3 August 2018 1:50 PM Shares Despite being rejected by CBS not once but twice , Freddie Highmore's Bates Motel follow-up show The Good Doctor has been a huge ratings hit for ABC, moving past The Big Bang Theory to become the most-watched series in America. The show follows a young surgeon with autism named Shaun Murphy (Highmore), who uproots his life in Casper, Wyoming to work at a prestigious San Jose, California hospital. " The Good Doctor 's message of inclusiveness resonates with our viewers and is a hallmark of what we aim to accomplish here at ABC," ABC president Channing Dungey said when announcing that season two would definitely be happening. Advertisement - Continue Reading Below "This series has quickly joined the zeitgeist and broken records in the competitive television landscape. We are thrilled to announce that Dr Shaun Murphy will be back for another season." But when will it return, and what will it be about? Here's our diagnosis... The Good Doctor season two air date: When will it be on? © ABC/Jack Rowand The Good Doctor season 2 will premiere on Monday, September 24 at 10/9c on ABC in the US. Wahoo! What about the UK, though? Glad you asked! The Good Doctor season two UK: When will we get it? Sky Living airs the show , and we'd expect that to continue later this year. Expect The Good Doctor season two to start airing around a month after the show returns to ABC. The UK's one of 200 territories that Sony Pictures Television (SPT) has sold it to. SPT president of worldwide distribution Keith Le Goy says it has become "the number one drama in the world", adding: "It's the must-watch, must-have drama that our partners in the US and around the world have been clamouring for." The Good Doctor season two trailer: When will we see footage? ABC dropped the first trailer for The Good Doctor in May last year but, as of July 2018, we're still waiting for season two's first teaser. When it arrives, we'll drop it into this feature, so keep this page bookmarked for all the latest news. The Good Doctor season two cast: Who will be in it? © ABC Advertisement - Continue Reading Below Chuku Modu, who played Dr Jared Kalu, is not returning for the second season , following the season one finale seeing him receive an offer from a hospital in Colorado. Will Yun Lee, Fiona Gubelmann, Christina Chang and Paige Spara have all been promoted to series regulars for season two. Lee and Gubelmann play surgical residents Alex Park and Morgan Reznick, while Chang stars as one of the hospital's attending surgeons, Dr Audrey Lim, and Spara plays Lea, the love interest and neighbour of Dr Shaun Murphy (Freddie Highmore). The last time we saw Lea, she had decided to return home to the East Coast after a road trip with the doctor – we're expecting her to change her mind, unless The Good Doctor 's about to completely change its structure for season two. (It won't). © FOX Lisa Edelstein , best known for playing Cuddy in The Good Doctor creator David Shore's other major medical drama House , will also recur as an expert oncologist who returns to the hospital after a brief suspension at the request of Dr. Aaron Glassman (Richard Schiff) to help treat his cancer. Speaking of which... The Good Doctor season two plot: What will it be about? © ABC/Eike Schrote Season one's finale ended on a note of hope. We learnt that Dr Aaron Glassman's (Richard Schiff) inoperable brain cancer was actually a low-grade glioma that can be treated. But don't get too excited – David Shore warned fans that Glassman is not in the clear yet. "To be honest, people seem to die on TV way more than they die in real life," he said. "For your regular characters, it seems to be the go-to place. "But Glassman's not out of the woods, and I think it's much more realistic and much more interesting and a greater storytelling opportunity to explore that journey of being sick. "This is a show about medicine, and we have guest stars come in and get sick and get better and whatever. How do our people deal with that? "How is Dr Glassman going to deal with his illness going forward? How is Dr Murphy going to deal with a sick mentor? What is that going to do to their relationship? What challenges is that going to present? I want to fully explore that." Expect this storyline to be a key aspect of season two. Want up-to-the-minute entertainment news and features? Boston Celtics - Simple English Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia Boston Celtics From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia Jump to navigation Jump to search Boston Celtics 2017–18 Boston Celtics season Conference Eastern Division Atlantic Founded June 6, 1946 History Boston Celtics 1946–present Arena TD Garden City Boston, Massachusetts Team colors Green, White, Black, Gold Owner(s) Boston Basketball Partners ( CEO : Wyc Grousbeck , Managing Partners : Wyc Grousbeck, H. Irving Grousbeck , Stephen Pagliuca ) General manager Danny Ainge Head coach Brad Stevens G League affiliate Maine Red Claws Championships 17 ( 1957 , 1959 , 1960 , 1961 , 1962 , 1963 , 1964 , 1965 , 1966 , 1968 , 1969 , 1974 , 1976 , 1981 , 1984 , 1986 , 2008 ) Conference titles 21 ( 1957 , 1958 , 1959 , 1960 , 1961 , 1962 , 1963 , 1964 , 1965 , 1966 , 1968 , 1969 , 1974 , 1976 , 1981 , 1984 , 1985 , 1986 , 1987 , 2008 , 2010 ) Division titles 21 ( 1972 , 1973 , 1974 , 1975 , 1976 , 1980 , 1981 , 1982 , 1984 , 1985 , 1986 , 1987 , 1988 , 1991 , 1992 , 2005 , 2008 , 2009 , 2010 , 2011 , 2012 ) Retired numbers 21 ( 00 , 1 , 2 , 3 , 6 , 10 , 14 , 15 , 16 , 17 , 18 , 19 , 21 , 22 , 23 , 24 , 25 , 31 , 32 , 33 , 34 , 35 ) Official website celtics.com Home Away The Boston Celtics are a professional basketball team. They play their home games in the TD Garden in Boston, Massachusetts . They are a part of the National Basketball Association (NBA) and play in the Atlantic Division. Contents 1 History 2 Rival Teams 2.1 Celtics-Lakers Rivalry 3 References History [ change | change source ] The Celtics were one of the first NBA teams. Since their start in 1946, they have been one of the most successful teams, winning seventeen NBA championships, including eight championships in a row at one point. [1] The Celtics' seventeen championships is more than any other team. The Celtics play their home games at the TD Garden . From 2008 to 2013, they were lead by the big three: Kevin Garnett , Paul Pierce and Rajon Rondo . But in July 2013, Garnett and Pierce were traded to the Brooklyn Nets , [2] and Rondo was traded to the Dallas Mavericks . Rival Teams [ change | change source ] Celtics-Lakers Rivalry [ change | change source ] The rivalry between the Boston Celtics and Los Angeles Lakers is two most of the best franchises in NBA history. It has been called the NBA's best rivalry. [3] The two teams have met a record twelve times in the NBA Finals , starting with their first Finals meeting in 1959 . They would go on to win the league in the 1960s and the 1980s, playing each other six times in the 1960s and three times in the 1980s. The rivalry had been less known since the retirements of Magic Johnson and Larry Bird in the early 1990s, but in 2008 it happened again when the Celtics and Lakers met in the Finals for the first time since 1987, with the Celtics winning the series in six games. They played once again in the 2010 NBA Finals which the Lakers won in seven games. The two teams have won the two highest numbers of championships, the Celtics 17, the Lakers 16; together, the 33 championships account for almost half of the 67 championships in NBA history. Photograph: Jeff Montgomery/AP A white Dallas police officer accused of fatally shooting her black neighbor inside his own apartment has been dismissed, the police department announced on Monday. Dallas: calls for justice after police officer kills man in his own home Read more Dallas police fired Officer Amber Guyger weeks after she fatally shot 26-year-old Botham Jean inside his own apartment on 6 September. Court records show Guyger said she thought she had encountered a burglar inside her own home. Guyger was arrested on a preliminary charge of manslaughter days after the shooting. She is out on bond. Jean family attorneys and protesters called for her firing following the shooting. Guyger was a four-year veteran. She told investigators that she had just ended a shift when she returned in uniform to the South Side Flats apartment complex. Sign up for the US morning briefing When she put her key in the apartment door, which was unlocked and slightly ajar, it opened, the affidavit said. Inside, the lights were off, and she saw a figure in the darkness that cast a large silhouette across the room, according to the officer’s account. She called 911. When asked where she was, she returned to the front door to see she was in the wrong unit, according to the affidavit. The 911 tapes have not been released.
